slides.body: Music already aired. Already used. Fees already paid.
But he had never registered publishing before the licences were signed.
Estimated performance royalties gone: over ₦800K. Uncollectable. Permanently.
Here's the publishing rights breakdown nobody teaches in Lagos. ↓

WHAT PUBLISHING RIGHTS ACTUALLY CONTROL:
→ Who collects your performance royalties every time the content airs
→ Who approves sync licence requests — if they can say no, they can block placements
→ Who shares in your sync fee (publishers take a cut — know the percentage)
→ How your music can be used after the deal is signed THE MOST COMMON MISTAKE:
Signing a label deal that includes a publishing clause without understanding it.
You sign for distribution.
The small print gives them 50% of your publishing income — including sync. WHAT TO PROTECT:
→ Self-publish where possible: register directly with COSON, SAMRO, PRS
→ If signing a publishing deal: keep sync approval rights in your name
→ Document splits with co-writers before any deal reaches the table
→ Register every finished track before any placement conversation starts One sync fee pays once.
Published royalties from a single placement can pay for years. Don't leave the second half of the deal unregistered. #MusicPublishing #AfricanComposers #SyncLicensing #SyncMaster
